entry.title: Re: Generate Time Forms from Time Admin Workbench
entry.content: How can we edit the email text?

entry.title: Re: Practical SQL: Common time saving queries to simplify your HANA workflow
entry.content:
Great topic and useful share!  Thanks.   

Just curious where do you store your community SQL Scripts for your team?  Thanks

entry.title: Re: AATP Product Allocation table in Fiscal Period instea...
entry.content:
Hello Roberto,

you can influence the dates when a month starts via the factory calendar. You have to create a specific factory calendar which reflects the fiscal period start dates. Then you can enter the factory calendar when defining the product allocation object in app Configure Product Allocation. That way the system will consider the factory calendar when generating the allocation periods (time buckets). It is then possible to start a month not on the 1st but, for example, on the 4th or 6th of a month.

Another possibilities is to maintain the allocation planning data (time series) not via UI (Fiori apps), but via the existing product allocation APIs. With the APIs it is possible to create CVC and time series (periods and quantities). Here you are completely free to define your time buckets. That way you can easily maintain a period which reflects exactly your fiscal month. Note, that it is then not possible to use the Fiori app Manage Product Allocation Planning Data anymore. You have to maintain the allocation data always with the APIs. But since you get your allocation data anyhow from IBP, then you need to transfer the data from IBP anyhow via APIs to product allocation. So that's probably no restriction anymore.

By the way, SAP plans to deliver with SAP S/4HANA 2025 a new PAL feature which allows to define flexible time periods. With that functionality it is possible to define and maintain own periods in a completely free way via the product allocation Fiori apps.

Best regards,
Carsten

entry.title: Re: Develop an SAP BTP End-to-End Scenario Integrating SAP Datasphere, SAP Build Apps, and SAC
entry.content: SAP Datasphere integrates directly with SAP Build Apps. Check out the blog post for more details!

entry.title: Remaining Budget Migration
entry.content:
Hi All,

I need to migrate remaining budget for around 500 projects from excel sheet . All these project budget are on ECC system & need to migrate to S4 Hana private edition . We can not connect these 2 system ( ECC to S4 hana ) and subsequently need to download all these details from ECC & upload to new system ( S4 Hana ) . If we need to develop any custom program , can anyone please suggest any BAPI name etc .

Thanking you very much for your support as usual .

Best Regards

Mohammed

entry.title: Controlling BP Roles via Authorization Roles Without BAdI – Feasibility?
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,
 
We are currently working on implementing authorization roles, based on the content of a business requirement.
In our current setup, we are using a BAdI to control Business Partner (BP) role behavior. However, we are exploring whether it is possible to achieve the same control over BP roles purely through authorization roles, without relying on a BAdI implementation.
Our key question is: Is it feasible to control or restrict access to specific BP roles using only standard authorization objects and role configurations, without using any custom BAdI logic?
If anyone has experience with similar scenarios, or could point us to relevant documentation or best practices, your insights would be greatly appreciated.
 
Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: Discover the SAP Community - How-To Video Series
entry.content: How do you remove your own but outdated blog post?

entry.title: Re: Duplicate date in EDW - Stock /B4R/DIM50
entry.content:
without knowing anything about ‘/B4R/DIM50’ ...

you could possibly SQL-read the data of a DSO with its table at database-level
and so easily identify any duplicate elements

entry.title: S/4HANA, Display Line Items in General Ledger, export larger files than what Excel can handle.
entry.content:
Hi,

"Just wondering if there is a way to download larger files than Excel can handle from Display Line items in General Ledger as one unit? I do not get the option to export in any other format.

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Necessity for a Scenario Based Training Approach
entry.content:
Thanks for sharing your thoughts, @kiran_kodre ! I'm surprised no one commented on this since 2022.

I completely agree with your points. As they say, modern problems require modern solutions. Learning in 2025 can't be the same as 20 years ago. 

You're saying "SAP should invent new type of training" but it looks like you've just invented it. 🙂 These scenario examples look great to me. And I'm sure SAP is perfectly capable of producing such content. But they seem to be more focused on selling the latest and greatest vs helping real developers to solve daily problems in real life.

And what you're describing is widely known in education science as well. Quote from the first resource I found on Google:

"Unlike children, who often learn for future application, adults prefer problem-solving and real-world relevance. Their learning is more task-oriented and focused on immediate applicability."

I'm sure all SAP professionals can relate to that.

entry.title: Re: Organisational Data in SAP FSM
entry.content:
Hi,

You can consider using Regions and Planning Scenarios. 

Creating regions across your S4 Service Units (not all service organization is needed in FSM, only segregated individual work groups) can be filtered and replicated via configured flow in CI, FSM has respective API for this.

hope this helps.

OU

entry.title: CFL/SCFD_EUI - Custom database fields with compounding F4 help
entry.content:
Hi,

We are trying to create a new field say 'Storage location' in SAP GUI and enabled for "Custom fields and Logic" using SCFD_EUI. We are having a problem in defining the value help for this as we need to have F4 based on the 'Plant' field present in the standard app.

Please guide us how can we create search helps with plant/company code dependent in CFL enabled with SCFD_EUI. 

Thanks


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: CFL/SCFD_EUI - Custom database fields with compoundin...
entry.content:
Please see the following documentation in the Value Help View section:

Input Options when Enabling Database Fields for Usage in SAP Fiori Apps

And the following KBA:

3020389 - Custom Database Field does not display Value Help - Custom Fields and Logic

Thanks,

SAP Support

entry.title: Supplying Export parameters as Import parameters to a Remote Enabled Function Module
entry.content:
Dear community, 

I am trying to figure out a way to call a function module remotely with a client program (either with .NET RFC connector or with PyRFC). For now both do not work for me as I am unable to find the correct solution to my requirement.

Below is my issue : I need to call a remote enabled function module, let's say /MYFM. 
When calling it from ABAP for example the program looks like follow : 

DATA : lt_param1        TYPE xstring,
       lv_param2 TYPE  xstring,
       lv_devclass    TYPE  xstring.


lt_param1 = 'test'.
lv_param2 = 'test'.
lv_param3 = 'test'. 

CALL FUNCTION '/MYFM'
  EXPORTING
    lt_param1        = lt_param1
    lv_param2 = lv_param2
    lv_param2    =  lv_param3 .

  WRITE: / 'Function module called successfully.' .

 However if I try for example to call it remotely (FM is remotely enabled so that is not the issue) I get the error lt_param1 not found (RFC error: 20 (rc=20): key=RFC_INVALID_PARAMETER). 

The import parameters of my RFM are it_param1, iv_param2 and iv_param3.
Below is how the FM is called for example using PyRFC 

val1 = 'test'
val2 = 'test'
val3 = 'test'

params = {
            'it_param1': val1,
            'iv_param2': va2,
            'iv_param3"': val3
        }

result = connection.call('/MYRFM', **params)

Can you please advise ? 
Many thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: Firebase Authentication Error in SAP Build Apps
entry.content:
Hi

Any update on this?

entry.title: Re: RAP - create child with IDENTIFICATION_REFERENCE
entry.content: Hi, unfortunately this is not the solution for the mentioned problem, but otherwise your link was very helpful for another problem we had. The answer to the problem here could be a determination.

entry.title: Re: CAPturing the Future: AI & CAP
entry.content:
This doesn't create a "fully functional application" though. Like where does the data go here? What does this application actually do? It's not going to do anything with deliveries or materials in an SAP system.

There have been quite a few posts along the lines of "look ma, a generated app" but hardly anyone is honest about what this actually is and is not. I wrote about it here: https://boringenterprisenerds.substack.com/i/159298850/some-kind-of-magic

It's great to be enthused about the technology but there is no reason to over-hype it.

entry.title: Re: Organisational Data in SAP FSM
entry.content: Thank you mate for your feedback,

entry.title: Re: Organisational Data in SAP FSM
entry.content:
Thank you for your feedback.

Could you please share more details about this potential solution?
Any documentation or resources would also be helpful, as I’m not very familiar with these regions.
Thank you in advance for your support. Best regards,

entry.title: Exciting Update: SAP SuccessFactors Customer Training Courses are Moving to learning.sap.com!
entry.content:
We are excited to share that all SAP SuccessFactors customer training courses (Project Team Orientation and Administration) are moving to learning.sap.com, the newest SAP Learning platform, on June 6, 2025. Whether you are looking to learn a new skill or stay up to date with the latest SAP innovations, the SAP Learning site is your one-stop shop for gaining and maintaining SAP knowledge.

  • Available on learning.sap.com now – Project Team Orientation (PTO): The Project Team Orientation (PTO) courses for each product area are available now on learning.sap.com and the SAP SuccessFactors Administrator Learning Center (SFALC).
    • Project Team Orientation courses focus on core out-of-the-box functionality and prepare you to make the best decisions for a successful implementation. Since these courses are high-level, they are applicable for System Administrators, Business Users, Business Process Owners, Executive Stakeholders, etc.
    • On learning.sap.com, you can search for PTO courses by product name or course code.
    • You can also access PTO courses with direct links from this guide: SAP SuccessFactors HCM Training Materials Guide.
    • As of June 6, 2025, PTO courses will ONLY be found in learning.sap.com.
  • Available on learning.sap.com on June 6, 2025 – Administration: With the 1H 2025 release updates publishing cycle, the updated Administration courses for each product area will be found only on learning.sap.com (and no longer accessed on SAP SuccessFactors Administrator Learning Center (SFALC)).
    • Administration courses cover key concepts, features, terminology, and practice of necessary skills to successfully administer your software. Since these courses are in-depth, they are applicable for System Administrators and other roles responsible for the configuration and maintenance of a product in SAP SuccessFactors.
    • On learning.sap.com, starting on June 6, you will be able to search for Administration training courses by product name or course code.
    • You can access courses with direct links from this guide (currently the direct links to Administration courses lead to SFALC): SAP SuccessFactors HCM Training Materials Guide.
  • Available on SFALC – SAP SuccessFactors eXpert (SFX) exams: The SAP SuccessFactors eXpert (SFX) exams for each product area will continue to be accessed only on SFALC. 
    • SFX exams, based on Administration courses, test your knowledge and confirm your expertise as an administrator of your SAP SuccessFactors system.
    • After you pass an SFX exam, you will earn a product-specific SFX digital badge.
    • All SFX exams can be accessed through the SAP SuccessFactors Administrator Learning Center (SFALC) (S-user ID required to register), available to SAP SuccessFactors customers for no additional cost (included with your subscription).
    • For more information about SFALC and the SFX program, including access instructions, please see this guide: SFALC & SFX Quick Start Guide.

entry.title: Determine SAP UI5 Fiori runtime context in core ABAP logic and NOT under handler or odata service
entry.content:
Hello, Hope everyone is doing well. 

I am looking for a utility (class, FM etc) to determine if the logic is being executed withing SAP UI5 Fiori runtime context. Please note I don't have any control on the call stack from were the initial execution begin so please don't recommend/suggest using HTTP handler or odata service to get the context. 

I am using FM: GUI_IS_AVAILABLE to check if its GUI context and similarly, FM: WEBGUI_IS_IN_FLP and FM: ITS_IMPORT_CONTEXT to determine if a classic GUI is launched within FLP. Now I understand if not either it can be considered as Fiori runtime but sorry I don't want to take that approach and need something concrete that will help determine Fiori Runtime and also help provide the FQDN and the URL parameters. FM: ITS_IMPORT_CONTEXT is a good finding but unfortunately it only works when I access a Classic GUI app on FLP and not when a standard SAP UI5 Fiori App. 

Appreciate any feedback or recommendation to the problem statement. Apparently, most of the responses received from LLMs felt like they all are hallucinating and sorry I don't have Joule Chat enabled so can't ask Joule at the moment. 

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: Blog series on my CPI + Camel learning journey
entry.content:
Great series, thanks for sharing this!

"This figure above is taken from nice open sap course on abap unit testing to sum it all up

Btw, course was not migrated after they killed it, but who needs testing anyway."

Dam', this is straight up MurderedByWords material. 😅 Pitty about the course, it was a good one.

Cheers!

entry.title: Re: Exploring New SQL on Files Use Cases with SAP HANA Cloud QRC 04/2024 and QRC 01/2025
entry.content: @FelixFritz - I am not sure I understand what you are asking.  If you are referring to the ability to natively create a delta share in HANA data lake that can be consumed by a third party (eg. Databricks) in a zero copy fashion, you are correct that this is not yet supported.  HANA data lake does not provide a delta table/share catalog service yet.

entry.title: Re: pyodbc runs "select" queries very slowly when providi...
entry.content:
Maybe it's the thing were precompiled SQL statement (either via a "prepare" call or with ASE statement caching) has to use a different query plan because it can't optimize for a specific value of the filename.

Since DYNAMICPREPARE=0 in your connection string shouldn't affect calling existing stored procs (according to Chris), it sounds like that should be safe to use.

Also, try running the good query with "set showplan on" to see what index it's using, then add an index hint to the cursor.execute() call.  Eg.

cursor.execute("select * from files (index <myindex)) where filename=?", ['pgb.197901.bz2'])

Based on https://github.com/mkleehammer/pyodbc/issues/214 it sounds like a statement is automatically prepared when cursor.execute() is called, but then subsequent cursor.execute() with similar queries will automatically use the same prepared statement.   If this is true, and if auto-prepare on cursor.execute() is on, and if you execute a large number of queries that can't be recognized as similar, you stand the chance creating a huge number of unused prepared statement memory structures in the ASE server.  I had a large ASE server overload once because of this.

entry.title: Re: What’s New in SAP SuccessFactors Learning : 1H 2025
entry.content:
@nageshpolu :

As a Learning Administrator, we are currently unable to update the Line Manager / Supervisor field on user records in the Learning Admin page. After the H1 2025 release, this field appears to be greyed out and is no longer editable.

Please advise if this is a change introduced with the latest update, or if there is a configuration or permission setting we need to adjust in order to re-enable editing of this field.

Thanks,

Panikar

entry.title: Re: Update/Modify any Table data dynamically
entry.content:
Kindly use the Code button to format the code. It's unreadable in its current state.

I'm also not sure why in 2025 anyone would still need to use subroutines and the old ALV FMs. There is also no PAI/PBO in this example, I'd clean up the comments before sharing.

What we use in privacy of our own test system is no one's business but when sharing something publicly, I feel we should at least make an effort to clean up the code as much as possible and avoid using obsolete code and concepts. Please take a look at Clean ABAP at your earliest convenience.

entry.title: Re: Joule for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - A Comprehensive Setup Guide
entry.content:
Hello, the 'System Landscape' configuration step is necessary?, because when we raise the ticket in SAP they pass us the URL in the help to configure Joule in Private Cloud.

And these steps do not include this configuration. And I don't know whether to request it again or it is no longer necessary.

entry.title: What’s New in SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central : 1H 2025
entry.content:
In the series of 1H 2025 release blogs, my next talks about SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central. The 1H 2025 release brings a fresh wave of enhancements to Employee Central, focused on simplifying HR processes, enhancing mobile usability, improving benefits handling, and strengthening document management.

From event-driven automation in Position Management to AI-powered support through Joule, and important upgrades in Benefits and Document Templates, this release continues SAP’s commitment to delivering a more intelligent, efficient, and people-centered HR experience.

Whether you're an administrator optimizing core HR, or an HR leader improving employee services, these updates are built to support your goals.

1. A Smoother Mobile People Profile Experience

Feature: Enhanced People Profile in Mobile
Why it matters: Employee data is now easier to browse on mobile.

Image Source: SAP

The People Profile in the mobile app has been improved for faster navigation and better usability. Accessing and managing employee data on the go is now simpler and more efficient.

Few Use Cases: With the basic integration between SAP SuccessFactors and Microsoft Teams enabled, users can now initiate a Teams chat directly from the Profile Preview. Additionally, an indicator displaying job title and employment status is now available for contingent workers — a feature that was previously limited to individuals with multiple employments.

2. More Insights, Fewer Clicks in Full Profile

Feature: Expanded Full Profile View
Why it matters: See the full picture of your workforce.

Additional cards are now available in the Full Profile, giving users more visibility into employee details from a single screen. This update supports deeper insight and quicker decision-making.

3. Event-Based Position Updates

Feature: Position Follow-Up After Imports
Why it matters: Automation reduces manual data maintenance.

An event-based solution now handles updates triggered by Job Information and Termination imports. This ensures cleaner data and reduces manual follow-up work in Position Management.

⚠️Note: The follow-up processes after importing Job Information with the Termination event are the same as after a Termination Details import.

4. Smarter Admin Alerts for Position Management

Feature: Admin Alerts for Import Issues
Why it matters: Stay proactive with position-related errors.

 Image Source: SAP

Admins can now track issues in Position Management imports via Admin Alerts. This helps catch and resolve errors early to maintain data accuracy.

5. Digital Signature Placeholders in Document Templates

Feature: Placeholder Support for Digital Signatures
Why it matters: Enable signing workflows directly in templates.

You can now embed digital signature placeholders in Document Templates. This makes it easier to trigger signing processes automatically — a big step forward for digital document management and paperless HR operations.

Permissions Needed:

  • View, Edit, and Import/Export permission under Manage Permission Roles  Miscellaneous Permissions  Configure Digital Signing for Document Generation.
  • View, Correct, Create, Adjust Order, Delete, and Import/Export permission under Manage Permission Roles  Miscellaneous Permissions  Configure Digital Signing for Document Generation.Signers (Signers).

⚠️Note: In the previous release 2H 2024, the feature was under restricted availability. In the 1H 2025 release, it is generally available.

6. Enabling Benefit Level Workflow in the Latest Enrollment Process

Feature: Benefit-Level Approval Workflow
Why it matters: More precise control over benefit changes.

The latest benefit enrollment process now supports workflows at the individual benefit level, allowing you to route changes for approval based on the specific benefit selected. This adds a new layer of flexibility and control in benefits management.

⚠️Note: The new field allows you to control the triggering of workflows for benefit enrollment requests more efficiently, during open enrollment. This field has two dropdown options:

  • Group Specific: Choosing this will allow you to trigger a single workflow for all the benefits. This is the current behaviour.
  • Benefit Specific: Choosing this will allow you to trigger workflows configured at the benefit level.

7. Recording the Waive Action by the Employee for Benefits

Feature: Track Benefit Waivers
Why it matters: Gain visibility into declined benefits.

Image Source: SAP 

When employees choose to waive a benefit during enrollment, that action is now recorded in the system. This improves compliance and gives HR a clearer understanding of employee benefit preferences.

8. Benefit Summary in the Group Enrollment Workflow Request

Feature: Summary View in Group Enrollment
Why it matters: Approvers see the full picture.

A benefit summary is now included in the Group Enrollment Workflow Request, making it easier for approvers to quickly review and validate employee elections before approval.

Images Source: SAP 

⚠️Note: This feature is only applicable for the latest benefits enrollment experience. Benefits summary details is not visible when the benefits enrollment happens through the legacy experience.

9. Generative AI Empowers HR on the Go

Feature: Joule in SAP SuccessFactors Mobile
Why it matters: AI assistance just got mobile.

SAP’s AI copilot, Joule, is now available in the SAP SuccessFactors Mobile app. Users can interact with HR systems using natural language to get quick answers, complete tasks, and navigate employee data — all from their smartphones.

Final Thoughts

The 1H 2025 release for SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central delivers powerful improvements across mobile, workflows, position management, benefits, and documentation. From AI-driven efficiencies to compliance-ready benefits features, these updates continue to support a more flexible and intelligent HR landscape. Be sure to review these enhancements in your preview environment, configure where needed, and communicate key changes with your teams to maximize adoption.

For full technical documentation, visit the SAP SuccessFactors What's New Viewer or connect with your SAP support representative.

entry.title: Not able to create new vendor number with reference vendor number through program
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

I am facing the following issue while trying to create a vendor number using a reference vendor number via custom program (BP Transaction).

I used the method by passing data through the structure ls_data. Inside ls_data, I have included the vendor address data. However, I am still encountering the same error messages.

It seems like the data may not be correctly passed into the deep structure. Could you please advise on how to properly pass data in a deep structure for this scenario?

Any guidance or examples would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Re: Organisational Data in SAP FSM
entry.content:
you can check these documentation:

 

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_FIELD_SERVICE_MANAGEMENT/fsm_plan_dispatch/managing-regions-172.html?q=regions

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_FIELD_SERVICE_MANAGEMENT/fsm_settings_configs/planning-scenarios-settings.html?q=planning+scenerio

entry.title: SAF-T for Romania, adding additional fields in Analytics reporting
entry.content:
Hello experts!

I'm currently implementing the NON_RESIDENT SAF-T report for Romania, in DRC, and one of the current pain point for my user is the lack of some specific fields in the Analytics reports to review the generated SAFT xml file. As of now, we currently have 2 reports tied to it : PURCHASE_INVOICE_LINE_ITEM_A and  SALES_INVOICE_LINE_ITEM_A. And for in the sales line item report, for instance, we dont have the billing document number as an option (in BSEG I have the information under Ref.document field (AWREF)).

After some research, I'm suspecting that my only alternative would be to extend the report to a custom Z, create a new CDS view(s) (copy of C_RO_SAFTSALESINVOICEITEMQ) by adding this and the other fields she need, and then creating a new query to be added to the list of analytical options in that screen.

Wanted to check with the experts if this is the best approach for this and if you guys had any similar experience...

 

Thank you,

 

Fabio Nii

entry.title: EWM delivery qty update without selecting process codes
entry.content:
Hi, 

I can see the /scwm/pridi has Edit button, after pushing the button the column Item delivery qty become editable. Im able to change qty and save and QTY is change but in EWM only. LE delivery is without the change. I think its reason the EWM process should be to use process code (ie user go to prdi, select process code etc). 

Is there any way how to setup automatic posting to ERP during delivery save without choosing process code?  

Also same delivery type is shared accross multiple WH, any possibility how to setup the same but for specific WH only and keep other WH untouched?

Or the only solution is custom development? If yes, any advise for some ench. Spot?

entry.title: CCV2 Commerce cloud, I have a requirement that the dropdown (suggest-as-you-type) and the reference
entry.content: I have a custom values provider (palcementoptionsSearchFacade) which populates the values in the dropdown and also suggest as you type. But when I click on the three ellipses then the refrence search is not showing up, insted it shows drop down values

entry.title: FIORI Custom analytical query. Restricted measure for year don´t work as expected
entry.content:
Hi all,

please clarify the behevoir of custom analytical query. I want to have the difference between the actual amounts of two years.

I have created a restricted key figure "Actual Previous Year" with the user input field. And I see that the data in this column is empty if "Previous year" is not equal to "Ledger fiscal year". For example, if I add 2024 and 2025 to "Ledger Fiscal Year", I can use 2024 or 2025 in "Previuos Year" and see the data.

So it looks like the selection of data from the database is always done with the "Ledger Fiscal Year" and the restricted measure can only use the selected data.

Is this really so by design? I´ve expected, that the data will be selected for restricted measure separetely.

entry.title: HANA Database Migration: Handling Incompatible Changes to Existing Tables
entry.content:
HANA Database Migration: Handling Incompatible Changes to Existing Tables

Introduction

When developing applications on SAP HANA, one of the most challenging aspects is managing database schema changes over time, especially when dealing with production systems containing live data. How do you restructure your tables without losing existing data? How do you handle incompatible changes that might otherwise require dropping and recreating tables?

In this blog post, we'll explore a powerful but sometimes overlooked feature in SAP HANA development: Migration Tables (.hdbmigrationtable). We'll examine how they provide an elegant solution to a common problem, using a real-world use case from a coupon management application.

Understanding Migration Tables in SAP HANA

Migration Tables (.hdbmigrationtable) are a specialized artifact type in SAP HANA development designed specifically for handling data migration during application upgrades. They offer a controlled way to transform your database schema while preserving existing data.

These artifacts become particularly valuable when you need to:

  • Split tables
  • Merge tables
  • Change column types or names
  • Add or remove primary keys
  • Convert non-nullable columns to nullable (or vice versa)

In essence, they provide a mechanism to script complex migrations that would otherwise be impossible through standard deployment processes.

The Problem: Adding Draft Support to a Localized Entity

Let's examine a real-world scenario that demonstrates why migration tables are necessary. In a coupon management application, we faced the following situation:

  1. We had a Coupon entity with localization enabled for the description field via CAP CDS modeling
  2. The solution was already in production with customers using it via OData endpoints
  3. We needed to enhance the application by adding a Fiori UI with draft support

The challenge arose because:

  • The Coupon entity had a child entity texts that was auto-generated by CAP due to localization
  • Adding draft support via Fiori.draft.enabled annotation would work for standard entities but not for the auto-generated texts entity
  • Enabling draft for the texts entity required structural changes to the table, including adding a new non-null primary key field ID_texts
  • Simply deploying these changes would cause HDI container upgrades to fail when existing data was present

The Solution: Two-Step Deployment with Migration Tables

To solve this problem, we implemented a two-step deployment process using .hdbmigrationtable artifacts. Let's break it down in detail:

Step 1: Preparation Deployment

The first deployment prepares the database for the upcoming structural changes:

1. We add the persistence journal annotation to the texts entity:

annotate Coupon.texts with @cds.persistence.journal;

2. This generates a migration table file /src/OPCS.Coupon_texts.hdbmigrationtable with the current schema definition:

== version=1
COLUMN TABLE OPCS_Coupon_texts (
  locale NVARCHAR(14) NOT NULL,
  ID NVARCHAR(36) NOT NULL,
  description NVARCHAR(255),
  PRIMARY KEY(locale, ID)
)

3. We add the generated table to the undeploy.json file to prevent conflicts:

{
  "undeploy": [
    "src/gen/OPCS.Coupon_texts.hdbtable"
  ]
}

4. We deploy these changes, which effectively prepares the system for the migration but doesn't change the actual table structure yet.

Step 2: Feature Deployment with Data Migration

The second deployment implements the actual feature with proper data migration:

1. We add the draft annotation to enable draft support:

annotate OPCS.Coupon with @fiori.draft.enabled;

2. The CDS compiler updates the migration table file, but we need to modify it to ensure a safe migration. Here's the complete migration script:

== version=2
COLUMN TABLE OPCS_Coupon_texts (
  ID_texts NVARCHAR(36) NOT NULL,
  locale NVARCHAR(14),
  ID NVARCHAR(36),
  description NVARCHAR(255),
  PRIMARY KEY(ID_texts)
)
== migration=2
-- generated by cds-compiler version 4.9.8
ALTER TABLE OPCS_Coupon_texts ADD (ID_texts NVARCHAR(36) NULL);
UPDATE OPCS_Coupon_texts SET ID_texts = LOWER(
  SUBSTRING(TO_VARCHAR(NEWUID()), 1, 8 ) || '-' || 
  SUBSTRING(TO_VARCHAR(NEWUID()), 9, 4) || '-' || 
  SUBSTRING(TO_VARCHAR(NEWUID()), 13, 4) || '-' || 
  SUBSTRING(TO_VARCHAR(NEWUID()), 17, 4) || '-' || 
  SUBSTRING(TO_VARCHAR(NEWUID()), 21, 12)
) WHERE ID_texts IS NULL;
ALTER TABLE OPCS_Coupon_texts ALTER (locale NVARCHAR(14) NULL);
ALTER TABLE OPCS_Coupon_texts ALTER (ID NVARCHAR(36) NULL);
ALTER TABLE OPCS_Coupon_texts ALTER (ID_texts NVARCHAR(36) NOT NULL);
ALTER TABLE OPCS_Coupon_texts DROP PRIMARY KEY;
ALTER TABLE OPCS_Coupon_texts ADD PRIMARY KEY(ID_texts);

3. This script performs the following operations in sequence:

  • Adds the new ID_texts column as nullable initially
  • Populates it with properly formatted UUIDs using NEWUID() and string formatting
  • Makes the previously required columns nullable
  • Makes the new ID_texts column NOT NULL after it's populated
  • Switches the primary key from the old composite key to the new column

4. Deploying these changes successfully transforms the table structure while preserving all existing data.

Key Insights for Working with Migration Tables

Based on this experience, here are some best practices for handling incompatible changes to HANA tables:

1. Always Use a Staged Approach

For significant structural changes, consider breaking the migration into multiple deployments to reduce risk. This allows you to:

  • Prepare the system for changes without affecting functionality
  • Validate intermediate states before proceeding
  • Roll back more easily if something goes wrong

2. Understand the Migration Script in Detail

Auto-generated migration scripts often need manual adjustments. Pay close attention to:

  • The order of operations (e.g., adding columns before making them NOT NULL)
  • Data population steps (never make a column NOT NULL before filling it with data)
  • Primary key changes (which often require particular care)

3. Format UUID Values Properly

When generating UUIDs for new primary keys, ensure they match your expected format. SAP HANA's NEWUID() function returns UUIDs without hyphens, so you may need to format them like this:

LOWER(
  SUBSTRING(TO_VARCHAR(NEWUID()), 1, 8 ) || '-' ||
  SUBSTRING(TO_VARCHAR(NEWUID()), 9, 4) || '-' ||
  SUBSTRING(TO_VARCHAR(NEWUID()), 13, 4) || '-' ||
  SUBSTRING(TO_VARCHAR(NEWUID()), 17, 4) || '-' ||
  SUBSTRING(TO_VARCHAR(NEWUID()), 21, 12)
)

4. Test Thoroughly in Non-Production Environments

Always validate your migration scripts in development and test environments with representative data before deploying to production.

5. Include Migration Tables in Version Control

Make sure to commit your .hdbmigrationtable files to Git or your version control system. They are crucial artifacts for the deployment process.

Tip: When reviewing database migration changes, pay special attention to the sequence of operations. The order matters significantly, especially when changing constraints and populating new required fields.

Conclusion

Migration Tables in SAP HANA provide a powerful mechanism for evolving your database schema while maintaining data integrity. By using this approach, we were able to enhance our coupon management application with draft functionality without disrupting existing production data.

The two-step deployment process demonstrates how to handle even complex changes safely. While it requires more planning and understanding of the underlying database operations, it enables continuous evolution of applications without the risky "drop and recreate" approach that would lead to data loss.

For developers working with CAP and SAP HANA Cloud etc., mastering migration tables is an essential skill that will help you navigate the challenges of database schema evolution with confidence.

Further Reading

For more information on HANA migration tables and related concepts, check out:

  • SAP HANA Deployment Infrastructure (HDI) Reference for SAP HANA Platform: Migration Tables (.hdbmigrationtable)
  • Enabling Draft for Localized Data
  • Enabling hdbmigrationtable Generation for Selected Entities During CDS Build

entry.title: An error has occurred system message in GRC NWBC portal links
entry.content: The links within GRC NWBC portal are not accessible in Chrome browser.  Links such as Work Inbox, User Level ARA, Search Requests, Background Jobs are not accessible.  The system displays an error message as shown in the attachment.  There is no additional information available in SLG1 or ST22.  User has tried to clear cache and reinstalling browser.

entry.title: Re: Interest Posting is generated on the same date as as...
entry.content:
Hi Charmaine,

I am sorry for the late answer, perhaps it is already solved. If we do a migration on the first day of a month (1-3-2024) it means we must add the condition for the Net Book Value of the asset and the Consideration start date on 1-3-2025. Do you use the Net Book Value condition or the Net Book Value (legacy data) where you also create the RoU asset separately and link this in the contract before completing the valuation rule?

The RoU asset migration will take care of the asset postings and on 1-3-2025 the liability, interest, repayment and possibly the difference between liability and RoU asset value are posted.

If the question is still there, please notify me via this question. I will make sure to check more often.

Best regards,

Jeroen

entry.title: Re: Blog series on my CPI + Camel learning journey
entry.content: @Jelena_Perfiljeva oh wow... I mean, it's an honour to receive such a comment from you. Seriously, thank you, it made my day!

entry.title: Re: Requirement class for sales documents (Details)
entry.content:
Hi,

 In S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, not all configuration elements are exposed. We don't have any option to check the details behind all the configuration elements. 

Regards

Jai Srinivasan

entry.title: Re: CLM in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi Arianna,

Yes, the app Migrate your data can also be used to migrate previous lease contracts. The logic of this app is quite straight forward. You can check this SAP page: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d5699934e7004d048c4801b552f3b013/3899fe4d8a9c48cfa7cf10fba6072ec9.html.

The only issue is that a migration of lease contracts can be done via this app. But if you want to migrate existing lease contracts which already have a RoU asset with values, you need to migrate these RoU assets as well via this app. This migration will create the RoU asset as Legacy assets and the APC and Accumulated Depreciation must be added as well.

After both objects are created you must link them manually. For this no standard functionality is available yet.

Best regards,

Jeroen

entry.title: Re: CLM in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi Shrinivas,

Unfortunately the GL account cannot be downloaded in this way. You can use the CBC in the Public Cloud to download the different tables, like contract type, condition type, condition group, object type, flow type, flow type with account symbol and account symbol with GL account.

These tables can be connected to give you the information which postings on certain GL accounts are created from a lease contract.

I hope this helps you.

Best regards,

Jeroen

entry.title: Top tip for your HCM move to the cloud: Run SAP Readiness Check for SAP SuccessFactors solutions
entry.content:
Hello, SAP HCM users! Today, we're going to discuss a powerful tool that's gaining significant momentum in helping businesses transition their Human Capital Management (HCM) systems to the cloud. With over 500 customers having already run it across 48 countries, it's time to explore why SAP Readiness Check for SAP SuccessFactors solutions should be your top tip for a smooth cloud migration.

The Shift to Cloud HCM: A Global Trend

More and more businesses are moving their HCM processes to the cloud, and for good reasons—enhanced agility, AI-driven cost efficiency, improved user experience, and easier updates, to name a few. SAP SuccessFactors, a leading cloud-based HCM suite, is at the forefront of this shift, helping organizations manage their workforce more effectively.

Introducing SAP Readiness Check for SuccessFactors

 SAP Readiness Check for SAP SuccessFactors is an innovative tool designed to simplify and streamline your journey to the cloud. Here’s why it should be your top priority:

1. Comprehensive Assessment

SAP Readiness Check performs a thorough analysis of your current on-premise HCM setup. It evaluates data, processes, and configurations, identifying any potential challenges and providing tailored migration strategies.

2. Actionable Insights

The tool delivers detailed insights and recommendations based on the assessment. This includes a roadmap for the transition, highlighting areas that need attention and offering best practices for a successful migration.

3. Integration Readiness

SuccessFactors is designed to integrate seamlessly with your existing SAP environment. The Readiness Check ensures that your SAP landscape is prepared for this integration, minimizing disruptions and maximizing efficiency.

4. Cost and Time Efficiency

By identifying potential issues upfront, SAP Readiness Check helps you avoid costly surprises and delays. The insights it provides enable you to allocate resources effectively, making the transition smoother and faster.

5. Customized Reports

The tool generates customized reports that provide a clear picture of your migration readiness. These reports are invaluable for decision-making and can be shared with stakeholders to build consensus and support.  And better still, it's available to run at no charge, for any SAP customer with a support agreement.

The Success Story So Far

As of today, over 500 customers from 48 countries have utilized SAP Readiness Check for SAP SuccessFactors. This widespread adoption is a testament to its effectiveness and the value it brings to businesses undergoing the HCM transition.

Getting Started with SAP Readiness Check

If you're considering moving your HCM processes to the cloud, start with the SAP Readiness Check. Here’s a quick guide to get you started:

  1. Prepare Your System: Ensure your on-premise HCM system is updated and ready for the assessment.
  2. Run the Check: Work with SAP to run the Readiness Check and generate the detailed reports.
  3. Review Results: Analyze the findings, understand the recommendations, and develop a migration plan.

Ready to take the next step?

📌 Watch the recording of the recent webinar: How to prepare for a winning HCM migration with SAP Readiness Checks | Move to Cloud ERP | 25.04.01 on YouTube.

📌 Sign up for a free Customer Evolution Kit for HCM workshop to review your results with an expert SAP Services professional. During this workshop, you’ll gain personalized recommendations and insights tailored to your organization’s needs. Don’t miss out on this opportunity to accelerate your cloud HCM journey!

Conclusion

Migrating your HCM to the cloud doesn’t have to be complicated. With SAP Readiness Check for SAP SuccessFactors, you can navigate the transition with confidence. By providing comprehensive assessments and actionable insights, this tool empowers you to make informed decsions and achieve a successful cloud migration.

Join the growing number of businesses that have leveraged SAP Readiness Check to transform their HCM processes. Start your journey today and experience the benefits of SAP SuccessFactors in the cloud.

Stay tuned for more insights on SAP Customer Evolution and cloud HCM!

entry.title: Re: In SAP S/4HANA, what are the changes in the Real Esta...
entry.content:
Hi,

There are no real changes between ECC RE-FX and S4HANA RE-FX. The only difference is when using Lease contracts with Valuation. In that case the postings will change from an Account approach to a Ledger approach. This is also the case in Asset Accounting where the Depreciation areas follow the Ledger approach.

If we just look at tables, transactions, programs in RE-FX all is the same. Not all new Fiori apps can be used fully unfortunately but the old transactions are still available.

If you move to Public Cloud also the old RE-FX is still in place although it is named CLM now. There is less possibility to configure the system but a lot of Best Practice is available.

Best regards,

Jeroen

entry.title: Handling LiveCache in a heterogeneous migration scenario
entry.content:
Hello All,

As part of the heterogeneous migration process, we are required to export both the SCM instance and the liveCache.

Requesting your guidance on executing the report /SAPAPO/OM_LC_DOWNLOAD_UPLOAD in the production client, along with the detailed steps involved.

Additionally, please provide the prerequisite checks that need to be carried out prior to the SCM system (both scm and livecache) migration to HANA.

Source details
SCM Version:
SAP SCM 7.0 - 21 (01/2019) - SAP SCM 7.0
EHP2 FOR SAP SCM 7.0 - EHP2 FOR SAP SCM 7.0 - 23 (02/2019) - EHP2 FOR SAP SCM 7.0
SAP NETWEAVER - SAP EHP3 FOR 7.0 - 24 (01/2019)- SAP EHP3 FOR SAP NETWEAVER 7.0

SQL DB: Microsoft SQL Server 2017
MAXDB database :7.9.10.07
OS: Windows Server 2019

Note:
The operating system is changing from Windows to Linux.
Only the SCM database will be changed in the target system i.e ( from sql server to  HANA db).
LiveCache will continue to use the same database (MaxDB) in the target environment.

Regards
Appuru

entry.title: Re: ​ Help Needed: Adding Variance to table in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content: This is so helpful ! Thank you !!!!!!

entry.title: Prerequisites for migrating BW 7.5 SPS10 from SQL Server to HANA.
entry.content:
Hello All,

As part of the heterogeneous migration process, we are required to export BW system.

Please provide the prerequisite checks that need to be carried out prior to the BW system migration to HANA.

Source details

SAP BW 7.5 SPS10
SQL DB: Microsoft SQL Server 2017
OS: Windows Server 2019

Note: in the Target
The operating system is changing from Windows to Linux.
BW database will be changed in the target system (from sql server to HANA DB).

Regards
Appuru

entry.title: Re: CU50 in internal tables
entry.content: Moreover, when I launch call transaction with mode D, H, Q or S (Sy-Batch = X), I have a dump…..

entry.title: Re: Implementing SAP BTP Secure Login Service for SAP GUI - enabling SSO and MFA with corporate IdP
entry.content:
Ahh, it was my fault for not recognizing that in the parameter ccl/snc/namealias/value_1 you have to give the exact string of the DN which has to be replaced. It's a string replacement including commas and spaces and does not work with patterns.

Thanks Aniket!

entry.title: Re: Discover the SAP Community - How-To Video Series
entry.content: @Padmapriya go the options menu on your blog post and click on "Report Inappropriate Content", select "Something else" as reason and request the deletion of the content. The moderators will be notified and get in touch with you. Alternatively, you could also request to flag your blog post as outdated instead of removing it.

entry.title: Re: How to avoid a purchase requisition for a material on...
entry.content:
Hi,

 There is no standard solution to block a PR from being created manually based on stock availability. I would recommend checking if BAdI PPH_MRP_PURREQ_CFL or any other PR item level BAdI can be used to check for availability of stock while creating Purchase requisition manually. 

Regards

Jai Srinivasan

entry.title: Re: Email Notifications in SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation
entry.content:
Hi @aweb79,

This can be configured through Intelligent Event Processing. Please check the documentation in the SAP Cloud ALM for Operations Expert Portal: https://support.sap.com/en/alm/sap-cloud-alm/operations/expert-portal/intelligent-event-processing/iep-config.html

Best,

Moritz

entry.title: Budget report for Cost center with G/L Account level using s4/Hana live connection
entry.content:
Hello,

is it possible to create a Budget report for Cost center with G/L Account level using S4/HANA LIVE Connection for PLANNING purposes?

Please provide your valuable insights as Early as Possible.

Note: I know that for P&L report planning is done using import connection. 

 

#sacplanning 

#sac

#SAPAnalyticscloudplanning

#SAPAnalyticscloud

entry.title: Re: SSAM 2405 with Dynamic forms - Forms are not loading
entry.content:
Hi,

Can you provide the documentation used to implementation the dynamic forms in SSAM?

entry.title: What's the meaning of "DB: Fetch" versus "Fetch" in SAT? (also: Open)
entry.content:
I did an ABAP trace with SAT, and I see different lines for the same ABAP SELECT statement, I can't explain the differences e.g.:

  • DB: Fetch FMIT and Fetch FMIT
  • DB: Open ZXXXX_OP_HISTO and Open SQL ZXXXX_OP_HISTO

NB: my question is NOT about the difference between "Open" and "Fetch", my question is about why there are two lines for the apparently same operation, or differently said, what's the meaning of "DB" in front of one of the two lines.

Does it simply mean that the request is sent to the database? (tables not buffered or buffer bypassed)

In my case, I don't need this information, it's a little bit polluting the interpretation of the trace. The flag "database-related operations" in the SAT variant is always selected (see below; I'm not sure if this option is about the "DB" lines). Is there a way to remove these "DB" lines? Only by using the ALV filter?

Thank you.

Sandra

My system: ABAP 7.40 SP 23; Kernel 753 PL 1300.

entry.title: Re: HTML5 App is not visible in SaaS consumer subaccount
entry.content:
The issue has been resolved, as described in the GitHub issue below:

https://github.com/SAP-samples/btp-developer-guide-cap/issues/116

entry.title: Re: HTML5 App is not visible in SaaS consumer subaccount
entry.content: @Vedant_Srivastava It was caused by the capability being switched off in the us10 landscape, but it was resolved in March.

entry.title: Re: CU50 in internal tables
entry.content:
What are the screen numbers? (use the debugger tool)

What dump?

entry.title: Re: Cost Center for Manual Reservation
entry.content:
Hello,

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition, users can indeed set default values for various parameters, including cost centers. This can be done through their user profile settings, which can be accessed by clicking on the user icon located in the top right corner of the screen.

To set a cost center as a default, navigate to Settings and select Default Values. Here, users can specify default values for several fields such as company code, plant, and cost center. Once set, these default values will automatically populate these fields in relevant transactions, streamlining data entry and ensuring consistency.

Best,

Jens

entry.title: Report to show which assignment profile rule(s) apply to a user
entry.content:
We have some assignment profiles with many rules. There are ways for us to see/report on which users are included in an assignment profile, but sometimes it can be challenging to determine which rule(s) a user applies to.

I would really like to know if its possible to create a report through PRD that would show for an assignnment profile:

1) Which users are included in the Assignment Profile

2) Which rule(s) in the assignment profile are qualifying the user

 

Looking at the Data Dictionary I"m not seeing anything that would link the user to the AP rule, but was hoping someone might have some insight on any tables/attributes that might accomplish what I"m lookingn for.

This would be a huge help for us!

Thanks and appreciate your time.

entry.title: Re: What's the meaning of "DB: Fetch" versus "Fetch" in SAT? (also: Open)
entry.content: Buffering vs. not would be my only guess here with the difference. Are either of those tables buffered in your case?

entry.title: Reforma tributária, RVABRA nota 3556404, off set?
entry.content:
Os bcset da nota 3556404 entregaram as condições CBS3, IB3S e IB3M amarradas as chaves de conta MWC, MWD, MWE.

Contudo, não foi criada sua respectiva off-set, para mapping na VKOA, gerando a perninha do recolher e do a pagar, sendo uma pela OB40 e outra pela VKOA.

Acreditam que a alguma razão fiscal, ou foi deixado para ser feito Z a gosto do cliente? 

Se estiver sendo ignorante me perdoem, pois me chamou a atenção ao fazer o paralelo entre MW2 e a BRI, por exemplo.

Abraços.

Eduardo

entry.title: Re: NOT ABLE TO POST PO FOR NLAG MATERIALS
entry.content: Check your configuration for material NLAG and plant combination in material master --direct path -OMS2--select material type and then check qty/value update folder and ensure material has qty update indicator checked -- also double check if you have maintained required purchasing view and plant/storage location view. Another point is that the material is non-valuated -you have to use account assignment while creating the PO.

entry.title: Re: CU50 in internal tables
entry.content:
Here is the dump :

As for the data that I also want to recover, I think it is in the screen :

but I am not sure at all. I don't know how to find the right screen...

entry.title: Big memory consumption XSLT, why Heap is used? Best with ST.
entry.content:
I've got an XSLT transformation (not ST) which converts a big ABAP internal table into XML (serialization). It did a short dump SYSTEM_NO_ROLL due to something like 4 GB memory needed. 

Here's the memory consumption in the short dump:

I can reproduce the issue in my DEV system with a small test program with dummy data:

I can solve the issue by creating an equivalent ST transformation, which is consuming less memory (below), and is also 10 times faster.

If I reduce the volume, XSLT is successful, but I see that it's consuming the HEAP memory (which I guess is bad),

while ST is not consuming it.

My question is, I'd like to understand why the Heap memory is consumed by XSLT although the total memory is not so much solicited (284 MB + 631 MB) and there's no other activity in the system, so I'm interested by any information.

Thank you.

Sandra

System: ABAP 7.40 SP 23; Kernel 753 PL 1300.

NB: I guess that Heap is bad because the workprocess becomes PRIV, so it cannot be reused by other ABAP tasks, and it's especially a problem if the program is running in a DIA workprocess. I don't know for BGD workprocess (my case).

NB: for generating the XSLT short dump, the internal table contains more than 260.000 lines, 20 fields of total 1032 bytes, including characters (Unicode 2 bytes), packed, NUMC, dates, no references, and two nested internal tables, the first one containing 1 line (always) and having 4 fields of total 186 bytes, the second one containing 1 line (always) having 20 fields of total 616 bytes including one nested internal table containing 1 line (always) having 4 fields of total 36 bytes. Via the ST transformation, the generated XML file is 413 MB.

entry.title: Re: CU50 in internal tables
entry.content: There's no dump attached.

entry.title: Re: CU50 in internal tables
entry.content: you were too fast 🙂

entry.title: Re: What's the meaning of "DB: Fetch" versus "Fetch" in SAT? (also: Open)
entry.content: No, these two tables are not buffered, and I think that's why we see these "DB" lines. But I guess it can happen to buffered tables if their content is updated, or after the restart of the application server, or after the buffers are manually emptied (/$sync).

entry.title: Re: Anniversary Tile for Managers
entry.content: I have another question regarding the Anniversary card. I had a leader request that the # of years also appear on the reminder card. Is there an option for this?

entry.title: Re: Big memory consumption XSLT, why Heap is used? Best w...
entry.content:
Hi Sandra,

I think it has to do with the following as described in the help for simple transformation - 

  • ST programs only have serial access to the XML data and are therefore very efficient even with large data volumes. The nodes in XML data, however, cannot be accessed in an arbitrary order.

In the help reference to XSLT in the same link it displays a diagram showing the asXML intermediate format when XSLT is used along with a basic description of the processing, which I would assume would account for additional memory usage (both links included below).

ST and XSLT links

 

Regards,

Ryan Crosby

entry.title: Suppliers shouldn't be able to enter negative values via credit memos when invoicing in BN
entry.content: Is there a way to prevent suppliers from entering negative values ​​when creating a credit memo or correcting an invoice?

entry.title: Re: Can MTA integrated with HashiCorp Vault?
entry.content:
I think I have a two part answer.

1. I would put all secrets into the destination and keep them out of the mta altogether.  

2. If you need something special, You should be able to use the custom builder functionality (i.e. a custom node app) to create a Mta config file that you can use to drive MTA action: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_HANA_PLATFORM/cf8b4c5847374960a68b55cb86eae013/4486ada1af824aadaf56baebc93d0256.html

Full disclosure, I have used teh custom builder function but not the custom mta file creation.

entry.title: Re: How to override a custom action after submitting in F...
entry.content: Have a look at 'Side Effects'. That did the trick for me.

entry.title: Re: PO - PurchaseOrderStatusNotification_Out
entry.content:
Hi,
In our system version (2020 FPS02), there is a configuration in the SPRO used to manage the activation of this PO response API: Integration with Other SAP Components -> Integration for Purchasing -> Basic Settings -> Define Communication Systems for Outbound Messages
You may want to check here.
Hope it helps!
Alex

entry.title: Re: Data records were not saved as no selection date was available
entry.content:
Hi,

I have added the fields VALID_FROM, VALID_TO  in the DANGEROUSGOODDATA, DANGEROUSGOODPACK, and DANGEROUSGOODRANGE parameters, but I still receive the same error message: 'Data records were not saved as no selection date was available'. Are there any other parameters I should check or any specific considerations to ensure the BAPI works correctly?

Thank you for your support

entry.title: Re: Fiori RAP: Define Sort Order for Value Help
entry.content: I've tried both of your suggestions. I added the annotation in the Metadata Extension of the CDS view and also applied the cast conversion for the "Status" field directly in the CDS view. Unfortunately, neither change made a difference. I really appreciate your help with this.

entry.title: Re: Fiori RAP: Define Sort Order for Value Help
entry.content:
Cast conversion in the CDS View for Value Help:

cast( cast( cast( domvalue_l as abap.sstring(10) ) as abap.numc(2) ) as abap.int1 ) as Status

Annotation in the Metadata Extension:

@ui:{presentationVariant:[{sortOrder:[{by:'Status',direction:#ASC}]}]

entry.title: Re: Update a translated simulation on Producer without to...
entry.content:
Hi Justin,

No, there is no way to do this automatically, the entire Simulation is retranslated.  So I would:

1. Make the changes to the EN Simulation
2. Duplicate it to create a second EN Simulation and move it into a different folder
3. Translate the second EN Simulation to the create 6 new Simulations
4. Open each of the new translated versions of the Simulations and copy the text of a macro that has been modified
5. Open the original translated version for the same language and paste the updated text
6. After updating all the macros in each of the original Simulations, delete the content created in Step 2 and 3

Regards
Shane

entry.title: Re: Why can't I configure Generative AI Hub in AI Launch...
entry.content:
As per this github url, it has mentioned this below note.

Note: Your can only call AI Core deployments, which you have successfully created before you start Jupyter Notebook. Restart Jupyter Notebook, if you made any changes to deployments in between.

Note: This will only work, if you have already configured and deployed the gpt-35-turbo model in your Generative AI Hub!

So, it means that we are expected to enable Generative AI Hub before we use this mentioned repo. However, the query was related to how to enable Generative AI Hub firsthand.

entry.title: Slow GOS export via CMIS interface (BTP / Document Management)
entry.content:
We connected an external Document Management System (DMS) via CMIS interface (BTP / Document Management).

But we receive SAP GOS documents only every 8 hours in the external file system.File attachments are transferred in real time. I can see them in the file system right after attaching them in SAP.

How can we accelerate the export of GOS documents to see them in real time in the external file system?

Is it possible to export GOS documents every 10 Minutes or faster?

entry.title: Re: Variable pay proration for employees hired before Apr...
entry.content: Thanks @xavierlegarrec. I will check the link and recording.

entry.title: Clearing date is missing in selection screen for Tcode S_P00_07000134 (FIORI and Back-end system )
entry.content:
Hello Experts, I need your help,

We have two SAP S4 Hana systems, there is one user who has "clearing date" field  when running the tcode S_P00_07000134 on FIORI on SAP system A :

but I don't have it when I run the same Tcode on the SAP system B and even on the SAP System A : 

 

Any idea please why this difference?

 

Thank you in advance!

entry.title: 🇵🇱 Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core
entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam event is on Getting Started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core and Python. You do not need any background in the topic, but you should have a lot of curiosity!

In this CodeJam, you will learn how to use the Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core to implement retrieval-augmented generation (RAG). This approach improves the responses of large language models (LLMs) and reduces hallucinations. You will learn how to deploy an LLM and the orchestration service on SAP AI Core, and how to query it via SAP AI Launchpad and the Python SDK. Additionally, you will gain insights into key generative AI concepts and learn how to enhance RAG responses.

This is a free in-person event and is planned for Friday, May 16th in Wrocław, Poland 🇵🇱 from 10:00 to 16:00 CEST. The language of the event and the content will be in English. The instructor is Developer Advocate @Vitaliy-R from SAP.

The event is kindly hosted by Capgemini. Thank you!

Legnicka 48H, 54-202 Wroclaw, Poland
Floor 5, Room 5.06 DONE

Please note that spaces are limited; we can only accommodate 25 attendees. Register here at the event page using the "Will you be attending?" functionality.

Prerequisites: Any Chromium-based web browser, such as Vivaldi, Brave, Microsoft Edge, Google Chrome etc.

Visit the SAP CodeJam group for more information on SAP CodeJam events, to ask questions, and to chat with your fellow attendees.

entry.title: Re: Key User Extensibility in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Sales
entry.content: Hi Jousa, Please check field UnderlyingPurchaseOrderItem in the Item section of JSON body. Hope it will work.

entry.title: Re: New AWS option for SAP ODP data extraction through AWS Glue
entry.content: So @MarioDeFelipe so can we officially recommend Appflow or AWS Glue for data extraction via odata to clients without worrying about ODP unpermitted usage?

entry.title: Re: Documentation of the XML structure of the Calculation view
entry.content: Only the no-code UI-based definition of HANA calc views is available externally. If you want to itegrate it with your solution, then you would need to contact pls@sap.com (SAP Partner Innovation Lifecycle Services). Regards.

entry.title: Re: How to Configure Email Notifications in SAP BW Bridge
entry.content:
Much needed!!!

Thank you, Dominik

entry.title: How to transport a G/L Account to a PROD Tenant?
entry.content:
How to transport a G/L Account to a PROD Tenant?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to transport a G/L Account to a PROD Tenant?
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community, 

 

In order to transport a G/L Account to a PROD tenant you can refer to the below SAP Help Portal Documentation for details:

Synchronize G/L Account Master Data

 

I hope this helps!

 

Best Regards,

SAP Support

entry.title: Re: New End User File Upload in SAP Analytics Cloud QRC3 2024
entry.content:
Hi Gerd,

This is a great detail, thanks for this, but once we created upload job when loader file skipping any of the info from mapping its failing.

 The structure of the selected file is used as a template for future uploads. 

in Template I chose date values data from 202505 to 203012, every time all periods data need to be entered, and users are not happy with it, as once the data uploaded later time they need to update records for fewer months but not for all the months.

Is there any work around for this, any help greatly appreciated as its effecting our budgeting data loads.

Thanks,

Lakshmi

entry.title: Re: Fiori RAP: Define Sort Order for Value Help
entry.content:
Consumption.valueHelpDefinition.presentationVariantQualifier: PUTYOURPRESENTATIONVARIANTHERE

when you annotate the field, you have to mention which presentationvariant to be used

entry.title: credit account has not been defined
entry.content: Does anyone got the error message: "a credit account has not been defined [message 131-58]"  in the outgoing payment?  I have a GL account link to the credit card but it still gives me an error? Any idea how to resolve this? Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Datasphere - Populating Dynamic Default Values in Analytical Model Variables
entry.content:
@jwschwab,

Following the SAP Datasphere upgrade on April 11th, we've also identified that the default value functionality for variables is no longer working as expected. This change is currently impacting our reports as well. We raised a customer message to SAP regarding this issue.

entry.title: Re: New or not to the SAP Community, share your story!
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am Josiane Laure, a Chartered Professional Accountant (CPA) and SAP Certified in FICO and Project implementation.

With over 18 years experience in Accounting & Finance, I started became my SAP career eight ago after being one of the key users in an SAP S/4HANA implementation. I was a consultant focusing on Finance modules and was part of multiple implementations. I am currently performing as SAP S/4HANA SME in Finance, controlling and project system areas, and as a Project Manager within a global private company. 

I am excited to officially join the community.

entry.title: Re: Changes Introduced in APIRule v2alpha1 and v2
entry.content:
Regarding the statement in this post:

"In one of the future releases of the API Gateway module, the APIRule CR will contain a new field internalTraffic set to Deny by default"

Does this mean that prior to v2, this will exist on the APIRule CR? (Is it available now?) Or is the intention that users will need to create their own policy for the release of APIRule v2 and that this field will be added in a subsequent release?
